Source   Edit  

Imports

spec, programs, maths

Types

PopMetrics = object
  core*: CoreSpec
  generation*: Generation
  lengths*: MovingStat[float32] ## accurate only after resetMetrics()
  scores*: MovingStat[float64]
  ages*: MovingStat[float32]
  immigrants*: int
  parsimony*: float
  validity*: MovingStat[float32]
  caches*: MovingStat[float32]
  bestSize*: int
  bestGen*: Generation
  bestScore*: Score
  staleness*: float
  usurper*: CoreSpec
  size*: int
Source   Edit   
Population[T] = ref object
  programs: seq[Program[T]]
  fittest: Program[T]
  ken: PopMetrics
  when populationCache:
    cache
Source   Edit  

Procs

proc add[T](pop: Population[T]; p: Program[T])
add a new program to the population Source   Edit   
func best(pop: Population): Score
the score of the fittest program in the population, or NaN Source   Edit   
proc contains(pop: Population; p: Program): bool
true if the pop contains Program p Source   Edit   
proc core(pop: Population): CoreSpec
Source   Edit   
proc del[T](pop: Population[T]; index: int)
remove a specific member of the population Source   Edit   
proc first[T](pop: Population[T]): Program[T]
return the fittest individual in the population Source   Edit   
func fittest[T](pop: Population[T]): Program[T]
the fittest member of the population Source   Edit   
func generations(pop: Population): Generation
return the population's Generation Source   Edit   
proc introduce[T](pop: Population[T]; p: Program[T])
introduce a foreign program to the local pop without setting it as the fittest individual, etc. Source   Edit   
func len[T](p: Population[T]): int
the number of programs in the population Source   Edit   
proc maybeResetFittest[T](pop: Population[T]; p: Program[T])
reset the fittest pointer if the argument is actually superior Source   Edit   
proc metrics(pop: Population): PopMetrics
returns a copy of the population's metrics Source   Edit   
proc newPopulation[T](size = 0; core = none int): Population[T]
create a new population with the given capacity and core Source   Edit   
proc nextGeneration(pop: Population): Generation
inform the population that we're entering a new generation Source   Edit   
proc parsimony(pop: Population): float
compute parsimony for members of the population with valid scores Source   Edit   
proc randomMember[T](pop: Population[T]; rng: var Rand): IndexedProgram[T]
return a random member of the population Source   Edit   
proc randomRemoval[T](pop: Population[T]; rng: var Rand): Program[T]
remove a random member of the population Source   Edit   
proc rescale(pop: Population; score: Score): Score
rescale a given score according to the distribution of the population Source   Edit   
proc rescale(pop: Population; score: Score; outlier: Score): Score
rescale a given score according to the distribution of the population; the outlier score may not be recorded in the population... Source   Edit   
proc resetParsimony(pop: Population)
recompute and set the parsimony for the population, if parsimony is enabled for the population Source   Edit   
proc score(pop: Population; p: Program): Score
retrieve the parsimonious Score for Program p Source   Edit   
proc score(pop: Population; score: Score; length: int): Score
adjust the score according to the population's parsimony and a length Source   Edit   
proc score(pop: Population; score: Score; length: int; outlier: Score): Score
adjust the score according to the population's parsimony and a length Source   Edit   
proc scoreChanged[V](pop: Population; p: Program; s: Option[V]; index: int)
inform the population of a change to the score of p at index; this is used to update metrics, parsimony, and the fittest population member Source   Edit   
proc toggleParsimony(pop: Population; value = on)
turn parsimony on or off; when switching parsimony on, this will recompute and set the parsimony for the population Source   Edit   
func usesParsimony(pop: Population): bool
true if the population uses parsimony in its score() routines Source   Edit  

Iterators

iterator items[T](pop: Population[T]): Program[T]
Source   Edit   
iterator mitems[T](pop: Population[T]): var Program[T]
Source   Edit   
iterator pairs[T](pop: Population[T]): (int, Program[T])
Source   Edit   
iterator top[T](pop: Population[T]; n: int): TopPop[T]
iterate through the best performers, low-to-high Source   Edit  

Templates

template qualityTrackIt(pop: Population; p: Program; s: Option[Score];
                        body: untyped): untyped
Source   Edit   
template qualityTrackIt(pop: Population; p: Program; s: Score; body: untyped): untyped
Source   Edit   
template withInitialized(pop: Population; logic: untyped): untyped
execute the body only when the population is initialized Source   Edit   
template withPopulated(pop: Population; logic: untyped): untyped
execute the body only when the population has members Source   Edit
